<h1>🚀 Open Python</h1>
<p>Python can be used in several ways depending on your environment. Below are the most common options.</p>
<hr/>
<h2>🖥️ Option 1 — Local Computer (Terminal / Command Line)</h2>
<h3>🪟 Windows</h3>
<p>Open <strong>Command Prompt</strong>
<br/>(Press Windows key → type <code>cmd</code> → Enter)</p>
<h3>🍎 Mac</h3><p>Open <strong>Terminal</strong>
<br/>(Applications → Utilities → Terminal)</p>
<h3>🐧 Linux</h3>
<p>Open <strong>Terminal</strong>
<br/>(You already know the drill 😄)</p>
<hr/>
<h2>▶️ Start Python Interactive Mode (REPL)</h2>
<p>The interactive mode lets you execute Python commands one by one.</p>
<pre>
<code>python3</code>
</pre>
<p>You should see something like:</p>
<pre>
<code>&gt;&gt;&gt;</code>
</pre>
<p>That means Python is running and ready.</p>
<hr/>
<h2>📜 Run a Python Script File</h2>
<p>If you have a file such as <code>my_file.py</code>:</p>
<pre>
<code>python3 my_file.py</code>
</pre>
<p>Python will execute the entire script.</p><hr/><h2>❌ Exit Python Interactive Mode</h2><pre><code>exit()</code></pre><p>You can also press:</p>
<pre>
<code>Ctrl + D   (Mac / Linux)
Ctrl + Z + Enter   (Windows)</code>
</pre>
<hr/>
<h2>☁️ Option 2 — Google Colab (No Installation ⚡)</h2>
<p>If you want to start coding immediately without installing anything, you can use <strong>Google Colab</strong>.</p>
<p>Colab runs Python in the cloud inside your browser.</p>
<h3>✅ Advantages</h3>
<ul>
<li>🚀 Zero installation</li>
<li>💻 Works on any device</li>
<li>📦 Libraries pre-installed (NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib…)</li>
<li>🤖 GPU support available</li>
<li>📓 Notebook style (great for experiments and learning)</li>
</ul>
<h3>▶️ How to Start</h3>
<ol>
<li>Go to: https://colab.research.google.com</li><li>Click <strong>New Notebook</strong></li>
<li>Start writing Python code:</li>
</ol>
<pre>
<code>print(&quot;Hello, world!&quot;)</code>
</pre>
<p>Press <strong>Shift + Enter</strong> to run the cell.</p>
<hr/>
<h2>🧠 When to Use Each Option</h2>

Situation | Best Choice
-- | --
Learning basics quickly | ☁️ Colab
Data science / experiments | ☁️ Colab
Offline development | 🖥️ Local Python
Real projects / scripts | 🖥️ Local Python

<hr/>
<h2>🧩 Mental Model</h2>
<pre>
<code>Python = Engine 🧠

Terminal → Direct control
Script file → Automated execution
Colab → Cloud laboratory ☁️</code>
</pre>
<hr/>
<h2>✨ Recommendation for Beginners</h2>
<p>Start with <strong>Colab</strong> for speed and simplicity, then move to local Python once you feel comfortable.</p>
<hr/>
